product_overview: Peek provides AI-powered, personalized home screen widgets by aggregating data from Google Calendar, Todoist, and weather, then generating beautiful widget visuals users can preview and add to their device. The web dashboard (Next.js) supports onboarding, OAuth integrations, widget previewing, and management.
core_goals: Make onboarding frictionless with email and social (Google) sign-in and a concise multi-step get-started flow.
Allow users to securely connect Google Calendar and Todoist via OAuth so widget content can be personalized from their data sources.
Generate context-aware, AI-driven widget visuals across predefined layouts and let users preview and manage those widgets from the web dashboard.
Provide a preview gallery and management tools to browse, select, configure, and refresh widget images before adding them to the home screen.
Surface helpful documentation and support via an in-app help/FAQ page to reduce support friction.

features.name: OAuth Login Flow
features.description: Multi-mode auth on the /get-started onboarding page supporting email/password sign-in/sign-up and Google OAuth redirect initiation.
user_flows: Navigate to /get-started -> Fill email input field -> Fill password input field -> Click sign-in submit button -> See successful authentication and proceed to integrations step

Navigate to /get-started -> Toggle between sign-in and sign-up modes -> In sign-up mode fill name, email, password fields -> Click sign-in submit button -> See account created and proceed to integrations step

Navigate to /get-started -> Click Continue with Google button to initiate OAuth redirect -> Initiate OAuth redirect via GET /api/auth/sign-in/social?provider=google -> Browser navigates to provider consent screen (external) -> See provider consent UI (external)

Navigate to /get-started -> Fill email input field -> Fill password input field -> Click sign-in submit button -> Receive authentication error message (invalid credentials) -> See inline validation and remain on login step

features.name: Connect Integrations (OAuth)
features.description: Post-auth onboarding step that lets users connect Google Calendar and Todoist via OAuth or skip and configure later.
user_flows: Navigate to /get-started -> Authenticate and reach integrations step -> Click Google Calendar connect button to initiate OAuth -> Initiate OAuth redirect via GET /api/auth/link-social?provider=google -> Browser redirects to provider consent screen -> See connected confirmation and Google Calendar appears in integrations list

Navigate to /get-started -> Authenticate and reach integrations step -> Click Todoist connect button to initiate OAuth -> Initiate OAuth redirect via GET /api/auth/link-social?provider=todoist -> Browser redirects to provider consent screen -> See connected confirmation and Todoist appears in integrations list
Navigate to /get-started -> Authenticate and reach integrations step -> Click Skip button to bypass connecting -> Proceed to widget selection step without linked integrations

Navigate to /get-started -> Authenticate and reach integrations step -> Click Google Calendar connect button to initiate OAuth -> Initiate OAuth redirect via GET /api/auth/link-social?provider=google -> Provider returns error or network fails -> See integration connection error message -> Click Retry button

features.name: Onboarding Multi-step Flow
features.description: A guided onboarding sequence on /get-started that walks users through login, connecting integrations (optional), and selecting widget preferences.
user_flows: Navigate to /get-started -> Complete login (email or Google OAuth) -> Click Next to continue to integrations step -> Connect at least one provider or Click Skip button to bypass -> Select widget layout preferences -> Click Finish -> Navigate to /preview to see generated previews
Navigate to /get-started -> Attempt to proceed without authenticating -> See prompt to sign in and remain on login step

Navigate to /get-started -> Authenticate successfully -> On integrations step click Skip button -> On widget selection choose styles -> Click Finish -> See onboarding complete confirmation and ability to access /preview

features.name: Preview Gallery (Widget Screenshot Preview)
features.description: A gallery at /preview showing generated widget screenshots where users can inspect, enlarge, and initiate adding widgets to their device.
user_flows: Navigate to /preview -> See gallery of widget screenshots (thumbnails) -> Click a widget thumbnail -> See enlarged preview modal with details -> Click Add to home screen -> See confirmation or instructions for adding the widget
Navigate to /preview -> Click Generate widgets button -> Trigger generate.batchHtml or POST /api/generate -> Receive generated widget images -> See updated gallery with new previews

Navigate to /preview -> Widget images fail to load (network or rendering error) -> See placeholder images and an error message -> Click Retry -> Attempt to reload images -> See images load successfully or persistent error message

features.name: AI-Generated Personalized Widgets
features.description: Server-side AI pipeline that combines calendar, tasks, weather, and context to create layout-specific content and styles, then renders HTML to images for preview and device use.
user_flows: Navigate to /preview -> Click Generate widgets button -> Trigger generate.batchHtml or POST /api/generate -> Backend fetches integrations data and calls AI -> Backend renders HTML templates and calls api/screenshot to produce images -> See generated widget previews appear in the gallery

Navigate to /preview -> Click Generate widgets button -> Trigger generate.batchHtml or POST /api/generate -> AI service rate limits or network error occurs -> See generation error message with retry option -> Click Retry to reattempt generation

features.name: Widget Management & Integration Configuration
features.description: Tools to browse layouts, toggle which calendars/projects feed a widget, and save configuration; changes update previews and are persisted server-side.
user_flows: Navigate to /preview -> Click Manage Integrations or integration settings for a widget -> See list of connected sources -> Toggle a calendar/project on via integrations.toggleSource -> Click Save -> See preview update reflecting the changed data sources
Navigate to /preview -> Open widget configuration -> Toggle a data source off -> Click Save -> Backend returns error saving settings -> See save error message and option to retry
Navigate to /preview -> Select a widget layout from the gallery -> Click Save or Apply to persist selection -> See confirmation and updated saved widget state

features.name: Help & FAQ
features.description: Help center page at /help providing FAQs and guidance for onboarding, integrations, and widget setup.
user_flows: Navigate to /help -> See list of FAQ topics and guidance articles -> Click a question to expand -> Read the answer and follow suggested steps
Navigate to /help -> Attempt to load help content but network fails -> See offline or load error message with contact/support link -> Click contact link to open support contact method

routes.description: Multi-step onboarding page with login, connect integrations, and widget selection

features.name: OAuth Login Flow
features.description: Login step on get-started page with email/password form and Google OAuth button. Clicking Continue with Google redirects to Google's OAuth authorization page.
user_interactions: Click Continue with Google button to initiate OAuth redirect
Toggle between sign-in and sign-up modes
In sign-up mode fill name, email, password fields

features.name: Connect Integrations (OAuth)
features.description: After login, step 2 allows connecting Google Calendar and Todoist via OAuth. Each provider has a connect button that redirects to the provider's OAuth page.
user_interactions: Click Google Calendar connect button to initiate OAuth
Click Todoist connect button to initiate OAuth
Click Skip button to bypass connecting

known_limitations.issue: Cannot complete full OAuth flow as it requires real Google/Todoist credentials
known_limitations.impact: Can only test UI rendering, button presence, and redirect initiation
